edges.evidence_quote: The TTP has publicly pledged allegiance to and fought alongside the Taliban
edges.context: TTP has pledged allegiance to and fought alongside the Taliban.

edges.evidence_quote: The TTP receives ideological guidance from and maintains ties with al-Qaeda
edges.context: TTP receives ideological guidance from and maintains ties with al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: Some TTP members have also been affiliated with the Islamic State – Khorasan Province
edges.context: Some TTP members have been affiliated with IS-KP.

edges.evidence_quote: the Amjad Farouqi group, one faction of the Lashkar-e-Jhangvi, ... merged with TTP
edges.context: A faction of Lashkar-e-Jhangvi merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: Jamaat-ul-Ahrar ... merged with TTP
edges.context: Jamaat-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: Hizb-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP
edges.context: Hizb-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: distance them from the Islamic State militant group's extremism
edges.context: TTP under Noor Wali Mehsud distanced itself from Islamic State.

edges.evidence_quote: Baitullah Mehsud, Hafiz Gul Bahadur, and Maulavi Nazir agreed in February and formed the Shura Ittehadul Mujahideen (SIM)
edges.context: TTP leaders formed the SIM alliance with other factions.

edges.evidence_quote: a group of TTP terrorists under the lead of Maulana Umar Qasmi broke away from the organization to form the Ahrar-ul-Hind
edges.context: Ahrar-ul-Hind splintered from TTP in 2014.

edges.evidence_quote: the Mehsud faction of the TTP defected from the main group to form a breakaway unit called Tehreek-e-Taliban South Waziristan
edges.context: Mehsud faction broke away to form Tehreek-e-Taliban South Waziristan in 2014.

edges.evidence_quote: hardline elements of the TTP from four of the seven tribal districts formed a separate group called Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan Jamaat-ul-Ahrar
edges.context: Hardline elements splintered to form Jamaat-ul-Ahrar in 2014.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Al-Qaeda IMU (pro Al-Qaeda factions only)
edges.context: IMU is listed as an ally of TTP in the infobox.

edges.context: Sipah-e-Sahaba Pakistan was an ally of TTP until 2018.

edges.context: Tehreek-e-Jihad Pakistan has been an ally of TTP since 2023.

edges.context: BLA is listed as an ally of TTP in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Group: ... Naeem Bukhari group
edges.context: Naeem Bukhari group is listed as part of TTP's group in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The TTP has publicly pledged allegiance to and fought alongside the Taliban, which has governed Afghanistan since 2021, but it operates independently and does not share the Taliban's command structure.
edges.context: TTP pledged allegiance to and fought alongside the Afghan Taliban, but operates independently.

edges.evidence_quote: On 10 October 2013, heavily armed Afghan Taliban attacked a Pakistani Taliban base in Kunar province of Afghanistan.
edges.context: Afghan Taliban attacked a TTP base, resulting in deaths of TTP commanders.

edges.evidence_quote: Again on 25 June 2016, Afghan Talibans and TTP clashed with each other in the Kunar province of Afghanistan.
edges.context: Afghan Taliban and TTP clashed in Kunar province.

edges.evidence_quote: Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan has close ties to al-Qaeda, sharing money and bomb experts and makers.
edges.context: TTP has close ties to al-Qaeda, sharing resources and personnel.

edges.evidence_quote: Some TTP members have also been affiliated with the Islamic State – Khorasan Province.
edges.context: Some TTP members are affiliated with ISIS-K.

edges.evidence_quote: Between July and November 2020, the Amjad Farouqi group, one faction of the Lashkar-e-Jhangvi, ... merged with TTP.
edges.context: A faction of Lashkar-e-Jhangvi merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: Between July and November 2020, ... Jamaat-ul-Ahrar ... merged with TTP.
edges.context: Jamaat-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: Between July and November 2020, ... Hizb-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP.
edges.context: Hizb-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: Some Sources also claim that TTP was behind the death of Nasiruddin Haqqani because TTP believed that Haqqani Network was behind the death of Hakimullah Mehsud as they disclosed whereabouts of Hakimullah Mehsud to US military in Afghanistan.
edges.context: TTP allegedly killed a Haqqani network member in retaliation for the network's alleged role in Mehsud's death.

edges.evidence_quote: There is clearly a symbiotic relationship between all of these different organizations: Al-Qaeda, the Pakistani Taliban, the Afghan Taliban, TNSM.
edges.context: TTP has a symbiotic relationship with TNSM.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2009 the Ghazi Brigade worked closely with the TTP during military operations in the Swat Valley, and the two groups jointly planned attacks on western targets in Islamabad.
edges.context: Ghazi Brigade worked closely with TTP in 2009, jointly planning attacks.

edges.evidence_quote: The TTP and the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan (IMU) have a long history of collaboration.
edges.context: TTP and IMU have a long history of collaboration, including joint operations.

edges.evidence_quote: The Punjabi Taliban reportedly developed strong connections with the TTP, the Afghan Taliban, Tehreek-e-Nafaz-e-Shariat-e-Mohammadi and various other groups based in the North West Frontier Province (NWFP) and the Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA).
edges.context: Punjabi Taliban developed strong connections with TTP.

edges.evidence_quote: The Punjabi Taliban reportedly developed strong connections with the TTP, the Afghan Taliban, Tehreek-e-Nafaz-e-Shariat-e-Mohammadi and various other groups based in the North West Frontier Province (NWFP) and the Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA).
edges.context: Punjabi Taliban developed strong connections with both TTP and Afghan Taliban, implying a cooperative relationship between TTP and Afghan Taliban.

edges.evidence_quote: The Punjabi Taliban reportedly developed strong connections with the TTP, the Afghan Taliban, Tehreek-e-Nafaz-e-Shariat-e-Mohammadi and various other groups based in the North West Frontier Province (NWFP) and the Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA).
edges.context: Punjabi Taliban developed strong connections with both TTP and TNSM, implying a cooperative relationship between TTP and TNSM.

edges.evidence_quote: Individuals and groups that are believed to have a supportive relationship with the TTP include: - Harkat-ul Jihad Islami (HuJI), an al-Qaeda-linked terror group
edges.context: HuJI is believed to have a supportive relationship with TTP.

edges.evidence_quote: Individuals and groups that are believed to have a supportive relationship with the TTP include: - Jaish-e-Mohammed
edges.context: Jaish-e-Mohammed is believed to have a supportive relationship with TTP.

edges.evidence_quote: Individuals and groups that are believed to have a supportive relationship with the TTP include: - Lashkar-e-Jhangvi
edges.context: Lashkar-e-Jhangvi is believed to have a supportive relationship with TTP.

edges.evidence_quote: Individuals and groups that are believed to have a supportive relationship with the TTP include: - Lashkar-e-Taiba
edges.context: Lashkar-e-Taiba is believed to have a supportive relationship with TTP.

edges.evidence_quote: Individuals and groups that are believed to have a supportive relationship with the TTP include: - Sipah-e-Sahaba Pakistan
edges.context: Sipah-e-Sahaba Pakistan is believed to have a supportive relationship with TTP.

edges.evidence_quote: Once they pledged allegiance to ISIS-K, they claimed that they were there to fight Afghan Taliban and Pakistan.
edges.context: Some TTP members changed allegiance to ISIS-K, indicating a structural shift.

edges.evidence_quote: The TTP has publicly pledged allegiance to and fought alongside the Taliban, which has governed Afghanistan since 2021, but it operates independently and does not share the Taliban's command structure.
edges.context: TTP pledges allegiance to and fights alongside the Taliban.

edges.evidence_quote: The TTP receives ideological guidance from and maintains ties with al-Qaeda.
edges.context: TTP receives ideological guidance from and maintains ties with al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: Some TTP members have also been affiliated with the Islamic State – Khorasan Province.
edges.context: Some TTP members are affiliated with ISIS-K.

edges.evidence_quote: Between July and November 2020, the Amjad Farouqi group, one faction of the Lashkar-e-Jhangvi, the Musa Shaheed Karwan group, Mehsud factions of the TTP, Mohmand Taliban, Bajaur Taliban, Jamaat-ul-Ahrar, and Hizb-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP.
edges.context: A faction of Lashkar-e-Jhangvi merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: Between July and November 2020, the Amjad Farouqi group, one faction of the Lashkar-e-Jhangvi, the Musa Shaheed Karwan group, Mehsud factions of the TTP, Mohmand Taliban, Bajaur Taliban, Jamaat-ul-Ahrar, and Hizb-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP.
edges.context: Jamaat-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: Between July and November 2020, the Amjad Farouqi group, one faction of the Lashkar-e-Jhangvi, the Musa Shaheed Karwan group, Mehsud factions of the TTP, Mohmand Taliban, Bajaur Taliban, Jamaat-ul-Ahrar, and Hizb-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP.
edges.context: Hizb-ul-Ahrar merged with TTP in 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: The Tehreek-e-Nafaz-e-Shariate-Mohammadi (TNSM) claimed responsibility for a 23 December 2007 suicide bombing targeting a military convoy on behalf of the TTP.
edges.context: TNSM claimed responsibility for an attack on behalf of TTP.

edges.evidence_quote: The TTP claimed responsibility for the 2014 Jinnah International Airport attack, which was carried out jointly with the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan.
edges.context: TTP and IMU jointly carried out the 2014 Jinnah International Airport attack.

edges.evidence_quote: The TTP Khyber Agency faction claimed responsibility for a 23 March 2012 bombing that targeted a mosque, run by Lashkar-e-Islam (LeI), in Kolay village of Tirah Valley.
edges.context: TTP bombed a mosque run by Lashkar-e-Islam.
